sections.paragraph:
καθήκω impf. 3 sg. καθῆκεν Jdth 11:13 (Aeschyl., Hdt. et al.) to be appropriate, come/reach to, be proper/fitting καθήκουσα ἡ τιμή the proper respect 1 Cl 1:3. Usu. impers. καθήκει it comes (to someone) (X. et al., oft. ins, pap, LXX) foll. by acc. and inf. (Diod S 16, 1, 1; Jos., Ant. 7, 131) οὐ καθῆκεν αὐτὸν ζῆν he should not be allowed to live Ac 22:22 (on the impf. s. B-D-F §358, 2; Rob. 886f).—τὸ καθῆκον what is proper, duty (Menand., Fgm. 532, 2 Kö.; Stoic wr. since Zeno [s. Ltzm., Hdb. on Ro 1:28; GNebel, Her 70, ’35, 439–60; MPohlenz, D. Stoa I /II 4th ed. ’70/72: index]; Polyb. 6, 6, 7; pap and ins [on freq. in both s. M-M.]; EpArist 227; Philo, Leg. All. 2, 32a) πολιτεύεσθαι κατὰ τὸ κ. τῷ Χριστῷ conduct oneself in a manner that enhances the prestige of Christ (dat. of advantage) 1 Cl 3:4 (cp. IG2 1365, 15f). παρὰ τὸ κ. (Diog. L. 7, 108; SIG 643, 6 [171 b.c.]; POxy 1203, 3; Philo, Leg. All. 2, 32b; Jos., Ant. 13, 66): παρὰ τὸ κ. τῆς βουλήσεως αὐτοῦ ποιεῖν τι do anything contrary to the duty imposed by his will 1 Cl 41:3. Pl. τὰ κ. (X., Cyr. 1, 2, 5 al.; EpArist 245; Philo, Leg. All. 1, 56) τὰ μὴ καθήκοντα (UPZ 191, 8 [III b.c.]; 2 Macc 6:4; 3 Macc 4:16) ποιεῖν τὰ μὴ καθήκοντα do what is improper Ro 1:28 (M-JLagrange, Le catalogue des vices dans Ro 1:28–31, RB 8, 1911, 534–49; against Stoic influence: M-M.).—On probability of a contractual metaphor Ro 1:28 s. Danker, Gingrich Festschr., ’72, 95f.—B. 643. DELG s.v. ἥκω. M-M. TW.

impf. impf. = imperfect

  1. sg. = singular

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et et al. = et alii (and others)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

impers. impers. = impersonal(ly)

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

oft. oft. = often

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

foll. foll. = followed, following

acc. acc. = accusative

inf. inf. = infinitive

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Stoic Stoic , various dates—List 5

  1. wr. = writers, writings

Zeno Zeno , the Stoic, IV–III b.c.—List 5

Ltzm. Ltzm. = HLietzmann, commentator and editor of various works

Hdb. Hdb. = Handbuch (in connection with a specific NT book, the ref. is to a commentary in the series Handbuch zum Neuen Testament, founded by HLietzmann)—List 6

Her Her = Hermes, Zeitschrift für klassische Philologie—List 6

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

dat. dat. = dative

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

RB RB = Revue Biblique—List 6

Gingrich Festschr. Gingrich Festschr. = Festschrift to Honor F. Wilbur Gingrich—List 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 491
